steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What do you say in the morning?
questions.hint: It's what you say when the sun comes up.

questions.question_text: What do you say when leaving a friend?
questions.hint: It's what you say when you are going.

questions.question_text: What do you say when someone helps you?
questions.hint: It's a kind word to show you are happy for help.

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👋

steps.title: First Day Greeting
steps.text: He says, 'Hello!' She says, 'Hi!' They listen and answer the teacher.

steps.audio_text: He waves and says, 'Hi!' She listens carefully. They are happy to answer.

steps.prompt: Say: I listen to the teacher.

questions.question_text: Who is she? (Grammar question)
What do you say when you meet someone new? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'Hi' and 'Hello' mean the same thing.
What do you do when someone is talking? (Reading comprehension question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Who are the friends playing on the playground?
questions.hint: Think about the video and listen for their names.

questions.question_text: What game are Ben and Kim playing?
questions.hint: They run and try to catch each other.

questions.question_text: How do Ben and Kim greet each other?
questions.hint: Think about what they say when they first meet.

items.definition: Used to refer to two or more people
Not old or not known before
Having existed for a long time

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 👥

steps.title: Ben and Kim
steps.text: Today, Ben and Kim join our class. They are new. Ben is happy. Kim is shy.

steps.explanation: Use he or she for one person; they for two.

steps.audio_text: Kim is new and says hello. They are friends now. Ben likes her name.

questions.question_text: She ___ called Kim. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'old'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: Ben and Kim are friends.
Who is new according to the lesson topic?
They are our ___ friends. (Vocabulary question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What letter comes after A in the alphabet?
questions.hint: It's the next letter after A!

questions.question_text: Which letter sounds like 'Bee'?
What is the last letter of the alphabet?

steps.question_text: Match the emoji to the word: 🔤

steps.title: The Spelling Game
steps.text: In class, they play a spelling game. She spells 'dog'. He spells 'cat'. They clap for the winner!

steps.rule_pattern: ___ is playing the game. Who is ___?

steps.audio_text: She says, 'I can spell the word.' He says, 'The alphabet starts with A.'

questions.question: What does he say starts with A?

questions.question_text: What is the first letter of the alphabet?
Who is ___? (Fill in the blank with a pronoun)
True or False: 'Spell' means to say or write the letters of a word.
Which word means a single character in writing?
___ is playing the game.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which word means when you say 'hi' or 'hello'?
questions.hint: It's what you do when you first meet someone!

questions.question_text: What do you say when you meet a friend?
questions.hint: It's a friendly word you say first.

questions.question_text: If you wave and say something to someone, what is it?
questions.hint: You do it with your hand and voice when you meet someone.

steps.title: New Friends
steps.text: They are new. Hello! My name is Tim. Who is she? She is Ana. Let's listen and answer.

steps.audio_text: Hi there! My name is Anna. I'm going to spell my name now. A-N-N-A. Do you want to try it too?

questions.question: How do you spell Anna's name?

steps.prompt: Say a sentence using words from this unit
steps.expected_text: Hello, my name is Ana and I can spell my name.

questions.question_text: They ___ new to the class. (Grammar question)
Who is new in the class? (Reading comprehension question)
Which emoji shows 'pencil'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: The alphabet has 26 letters.
What do you say when you see your friend? (Review question)
